skill.name: agentdb-reinforcement-learning-training
skill.description: Guides users through AgentDB reinforcement learning workflows, including environment setup, algorithm configuration, training loops, evaluation, and deployment patterns.
skill.summary: Build, evaluate, and deploy AgentDB reinforcement learning agents with structured training guidance.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography hits are false positives caused by reinforcement learning terminology and example metrics. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found. The skill should publish with warnings because it includes npm installation commands, a bash orchestration example, hardcoded documentation URLs, and production API examples that need user review before execution.

medium_findings.title: Executable Training Commands Require User Review
medium_findings.description: The skill provides npm install commands and a bash training script that executes local Node files. This is legitimate setup guidance, but users should review dependencies and local scripts before running the commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are inside documentation code fences and are not executed by the skill automatically. Risk remains because the instructions ask users to install packages and run local Node entry points.

medium_findings.title: Production API Example Lacks Security Hardening
medium_findings.description: The deployment examples create a prediction endpoint from request body input without showing authentication, validation, rate limiting, or sanitized error handling. This is not malicious, but it could be unsafe if copied directly into production.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is presented as an example, so legitimate use is possible. The omission is clear in the shown API handler and matters for production deployment guidance.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography detections point to reinforcement learning terms, episode counts, evaluation loops, and diagram labels rather than cryptographic functions or algorithms. No evidence of weak crypto usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed the cited lines are documentation, RL configuration values, or graph labels. They do not implement encryption, hashing, signing, or key handling.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Localhost References
low_findings.description: The network findings are AgentDB documentation links and a localhost API endpoint example. No evidence of data exfiltration, remote callbacks, or hidden network behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are visible documentation resources or a local prediction endpoint in an example. They are not paired with secret collection or outbound request code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell and Package Manager Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for installing packages and running a multi-step training script. These commands are user-triggered examples, but they should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are concrete and executable, but they are contained in markdown examples. There is no automatic execution path in the skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copyable Express Endpoint Without Access Controls
dangerous_patterns.description: The production deployment example exposes a prediction route without documenting access controls or input validation. This is a security hardening gap in example code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is an illustrative snippet, but the missing controls are directly visible and relevant to production use.

content.value_statement: Reinforcement learning projects need repeatable setup, training, evaluation, and deployment steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured AgentDB workflow for building RL agents.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use AgentDB reinforcement learning workflows.
Lists supported RL algorithms such as Q-Learning, SARSA, DQN, PPO, TD3, SAC, and Decision Transformer.
Provides a five-phase workflow for setup, configuration, training, evaluation, and deployment.
Shows example training loops, replay buffer configuration, monitoring, and benchmark steps.
Includes guidance for exporting trained agents and creating a simple prediction API.
Defines success criteria for convergence, benchmark improvement, latency, and production readiness.
limitations: The skill is documentation-focused and does not include a complete runnable project.
Examples assume AgentDB packages and APIs are available in the user environment.
Production API snippets require additional security controls before real deployment.
Training results depend on environment design, reward quality, data volume, and compute resources.

use_cases.title: Prototype an RL Agent
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to configure an AgentDB environment, choose an algorithm, and start a training loop.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Training Quality
use_cases.description: Apply the benchmark and validation steps to compare rewards, success rates, and inference speed against a baseline.

use_cases.title: Prepare Deployment Guidance
use_cases.description: Use the export, monitoring, and deployment sections as a checklist for production planning.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an RL Algorithm
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this AgentDB RL training skill to recommend an algorithm for my environment. Describe the state space, action space, reward signal, and expected constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Training
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline an AgentDB training setup for a DQN agent. Include environment setup, replay buffer settings, hyperparameters, and monitoring metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Convergence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my RL training results. Identify likely convergence problems, reward design issues, and benchmark gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production Deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a deployment checklist for a trained AgentDB RL agent. Include validation gates, API safeguards, monitoring, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan a first DQN experiment for a grid-world task.
output_examples.output: A phased training plan with environment definition, DQN hyperparameters, replay buffer settings, monitoring metrics, and validation targets.

output_examples.input: Review whether my trained agent is ready to deploy.
output_examples.output: A readiness assessment covering convergence, baseline improvement, inference latency, API hardening, monitoring, and rollback requirements.

output_examples.input: Compare PPO and DQN for my AgentDB use case.
output_examples.output: A tradeoff summary that maps each algorithm to action space type, stability needs, sample efficiency, and deployment complexity.

best_practices: Review all package installation and shell commands before running them in a project environment.
Validate reward functions and baselines before judging model performance.
Add authentication, input validation, rate limits, and sanitized errors to any production API.
anti_patterns: Do not copy the deployment API example directly into production without security controls.
Do not treat reward improvement alone as proof that the agent is safe or reliable.
Do not run long training jobs without checkpoints, monitoring, and reproducible configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill train an agent by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance and examples for building AgentDB RL training workflows.

faq.question: Which algorithms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Q-Learning, SARSA, DQN, Actor-Critic, A2C, PPO, TD3, SAC, and Decision Transformer.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the production API example secure as written?
faq.answer: No. Add authentication, validation, rate limiting, logging controls, and sanitized error responses before deployment.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it includes executable setup commands and deployment examples that need careful review.

faq.question: What should users verify before running examples?
faq.answer: Users should verify package sources, local script contents, AgentDB API compatibility, resource limits, and environment safety.
